name: Same Threat Detected on Same Network Different Hosts (by Machine Identifier)
notes: This rule triggers when the same threat has been detected on different hosts in the same segment of the network hierarchy. This could indicate the presence of a malware spreading in the network.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Network, Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Network, Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Same Threat Detected on Same Network Different Hosts (by Machine Identifier) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Network, Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Same Threat Detected on Same Network Different Hosts (by Machine Identifier)
Event description: Same threat has been detected on different hosts in the same network hierarchy.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Threat Name (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
